title: Oxystophyllum atrorubens
intro: Oxystophyllum atrorubens, also known as the Red-stemmed Oxystophyllum, is a type of orchid native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating skin infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Oxystophyllum atrorubens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Oxystophyllum atrorubens is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly referred to as the orchid family. This classification places it among the approximately 30,000 known species of orchids. The genus Oxystophyllum belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Coelogyneae. Further categorization within the tribe places Oxystophyllum atrorubens among the Coelogyne-like orchids. The exact classification may vary slightly depending on the source, but it is generally accepted within the orchid family.
